combat_history.event: Major boiler room fire during refit at Karwar naval base. Fire caused significant damage and delayed operations by several months.
combat_history.significance: Highlighted maintenance challenges and potential reliability issues with the aging propulsion system, raising questions about operational readiness

combat_history.event: Participated in Malabar naval exercise with US, Japanese, and later Australian naval forces, conducting carrier operations in the Bay of Bengal.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated Indian carrier capability in multilateral operations and interoperability with allied forces in the Indo-Pacific

combat_history.event: Operated alongside USS Carl Vinson (CVN-70) in dual-carrier operations during Malabar 2021, practicing complex air operations.
combat_history.significance: First dual-carrier operations between Indian and US forces, showcasing growing military cooperation and Indian carrier maturity

modernization.description: Planned upgrade to combat systems, radar systems, and aircraft handling equipment. May include integration of indigenous systems and improved defensive capabilities.
modernization.impact: Would extend service life and improve operational capabilities, particularly in electronic warfare and air defense

modernization.description: Comprehensive overhaul of propulsion system following 2019 fire damage and ongoing reliability issues with Soviet-era boilers.
modernization.impact: Critical for maintaining operational availability and preventing future propulsion failures

vulnerabilities.description: Aging Soviet-era boiler system has suffered multiple failures including the 2019 fire. Complex steam propulsion requires extensive maintenance and has lower availability than gas turbine systems.
vulnerabilities.context: Propulsion issues limit operational tempo and force structure planning, particularly critical given India operates only one operational carrier
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ongoing overhaul program and enhanced maintenance protocols, but fundamental system remains unchanged

vulnerabilities.description: MiG-29K aircraft have suffered high accident rates and serviceability issues. Limited to 24 fighters max, less than half the capacity of larger carriers. No organic AEW aircraft beyond helicopters.
vulnerabilities.context: Reduces strike capability and defensive coverage compared to larger carrier air wings. High accident rate impacts pilot training and readiness
vulnerabilities.mitigation: India considering replacement aircraft options including naval Tejas, but timeline uncertain

vulnerabilities.description: As India's only operational large-deck carrier, any extended downtime eliminates Indian carrier capability entirely. No backup while INS Vikrant works up to full operational capability.
vulnerabilities.context: Critical vulnerability for a navy seeking to project power across the vast Indian Ocean Region with multiple potential threat axes
vulnerabilities.mitigation: INS Vikrant commissioning provides backup, but full operational capability will take years to develop

vulnerabilities.description: Relies primarily on short-range Barak-1 and AK-630 systems without longer-range area air defense. Vulnerable to saturation attacks by anti-ship missiles.
vulnerabilities.context: Modern anti-ship missiles like the Chinese YJ-18 or Pakistani CM-400AKG pose significant threats that current defenses may not adequately counter
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Future upgrades may include longer-range SAM systems, but currently relies on escort vessels for area defense

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Operates as centerpiece of Carrier Battle Group with destroyer and frigate escorts, typically 4-6 month deployments in Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, or extended Indian Ocean patrols
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: 2-3 destroyers (Kolkata or Delhi class), 1-2 frigates, fleet tanker, submarine escort when available
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Availability rate estimated at 60-70% due to maintenance requirements and propulsion issues. Requires extensive shore-based maintenance periods.

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Thai carrier much smaller (11,400 tons vs 45,400) and rarely operates aircraft due to cost. Vikramaditya represents serious carrier capability while Chakri Naruebet is essentially a helicopter carrier.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Compare operational tempo and actual carrier capability utilization between emerging naval powers

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar STOBAR layout but Kuznetsov retains heavy missile armament and larger aircraft capacity (50+ vs 30). Russian carrier has had even worse reliability issues and rarely deploys.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Compare how two navies adapted similar Soviet carrier designs for different strategic needs and operational concepts

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar displacement and STOBAR design but newer refit, better maintained, and larger air wing (24-30 J-15 fighters). China operates two carriers vs India's one operational.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: India vs China carrier capabilities in the Indian Ocean - comparing operational patterns, aircraft performance, and strategic objectives

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Nuclear powered, CATOBAR operations enable larger/heavier aircraft, but smaller than Vikramaditya. French carrier has higher availability and more advanced systems integration.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: STOBAR vs CATOBAR operations - trade-offs in aircraft capability, operational flexibility, and maintenance requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Much larger (65,000 tons), STOVL F-35B aircraft vs STOBAR MiG-29K, twin-island design, newer systems but fewer defensive weapons. UK has two hulls vs India's one operational.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Traditional carrier powers vs emerging naval powers - comparing modern carrier design philosophies and operational concepts
